steps.description: Type the text 'contact exporter' into the search input field located at the top of the "Samples" tab within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code, to filter sample projects.

Click the "Create" button next to "Contact Exporter using Graph Toolkit" in the "Samples" panel of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code. Add ${{var:app_name:contact-exporter}} in to env.
Click the "Deploy" option under the "LIFECYCLE" section in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar of Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Deploy" button in the confirmation dialog asking "Do you want to deploy resources in dev environment?" within the Visual Studio Code interface, under the context of Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit provisioning.
@assertion there's "Ok to proceed? (y)" exist in terminal log.
Click the "y" input prompt in the integrated TERMINAL panel of Visual Studio Code to confirm proceeding with installation.
Type 'y' in the VS Code Terminal to confirm installation of @azure/static-web-apps-cli during Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit provisioning.
Press Enter key in the Visual Studio Code integrated terminal to confirm installation by responding to the "Ok to proceed? (y/n)" prompt.
@assertion there's notification: "actions in deploy stage executed successfully." exist
Click the "Explorer" icon in the left sidebar of Visual Studio Code to open the file explorer panel.
Click the "Open File..." option in the File menu of Visual Studio Code to launch the file picker dialog.
Click the "Home" folder in the left sidebar of a Linux file chooser dialog to navigate to the user's home directory.
Press the down arrow key to move selection from "AgentsToolkitProjects" to "Downloads" in the file picker dialog.
Press Enter to open the selected "Downloads" folder in the file picker dialog.
Click the "contact.csv" file in the file picker dialog within the Downloads folder, selecting the highlighted row in the file list.
Click the "Open" button in the file selection dialog to confirm opening the highlighted "contact.csv" file from the Downloads folder.
